“In Jeremiah 29 we find a letter in which Jeremiah delivered the word of the Lord to those already in exile in Babylon. To the exiles the Lord said, ‘Build ye houses, and dwell in them; and plant gardens, and eat the fruit of them’ (Jer. 29:5), all language reminiscent of Jeremiah's call ‘to build, and to plant’ (Jer. 1:10). The point is that the Exile would be long, and therefore the people…

Lehi prophesied that the Jews would return to Jerusalem after they were sacked by the Babylonians. This is a well described event in the Old Testament. Jeremiah said, For thus saith the LORD, That after seventy years be accomplished at Babylon I will visit you, and perform my good word toward you, in causing you to return to this place (Jer 28:10). The entire book of Nehemiah describes the return…

The formula for finding Jesus has always been and ever will be the same-the earnest and sincere prayer of a humble and pure heart. The prophet Jeremiah counseled, ". . . ye shall seek me, and find me, when ye shall search for me with all your heart." (Jeremiah 29:13.) (Thomas S. Monson, Pathways to Perfection, p.262)

May I refer to some of these prophecies briefly. There are many of them, not only regarding the dispersion of Judah and Israel but also the gathering. Jeremiah said: "Thus, saith the Lord of hosts; Behold, I will send upon them the sword, the famine, and the pestilence, and will make them like vile figs, that cannot be eaten, they are so evil." Then he tells how they will be persecuted and…

24-32. Shemaiah, a man among the captives in Babylon, apparently took it upon himself to write to Zephaniah, a priest in Jerusalem, asking him to reprove or punish Jeremiah for prophesying that they would go captive into Babylon. Zephaniah, the priest who received the letter, then read the letter to Jeremiah, which prompted his reply and prophecy against Shemaiah. This is the sixth prophecy…
